name: Endothelial cells from older human donors had more dysfunctional telomeres
summary: Human endothelial-cell data support age-related telomere dysfunction as a target-relevance signal.
statement: Human lung microvascular endothelial cells from older donors showed an increased abundance of dysfunctional telomeres compared with cells from young donors.
population_or_model: Human lung microvascular endothelial cells from two young and two old donors
quantitative_note: The source reports analyses of 64 cells from young donors and 57 cells from old donors.
caveats: The human sample is small and cell-based.
The result supports target relevance but does not test a telomere-capping intervention.
